GitHub App Setup Required

Hive needs a GitHub App to authenticate. Choose an option:

Use the public Hive App (quickest)

Dashboard login only. Agents can read repos but cannot create issues, PRs, or merge.

Create your own GitHub App (recommended for full ACMM)

Full agent capabilities: issues, PRs, auto-merge. Required for ACMM levels 3-6.

Quick setup:
  1. Open hive.yaml on the host (mounted at /etc/hive/hive.yaml inside the container)
  2. Add to the github: section:
    oauth_client_id: Ov23ligE2p0gjXg6xAUf
  3. Restart the container: docker compose restart hive
  4. Click "Sign in with GitHub" again
Create a GitHub App:
  1. Go to github.com/settings/apps/new (or your org's Settings → Developer settings → GitHub Apps)
  2. Set the app name (e.g. my-hive)
  3. Homepage URL: any URL (e.g. https://github.com/kubestellar/hive)
  4. Uncheck "Webhook → Active" (not needed)
  5. Under "Device Flow", check Enable Device Flow
  6. Set the permissions below, then click "Create GitHub App"
  7. On the app page, note the App ID and Client ID
  8. Generate a private key (.pem file) and save it to ./secrets/gh-app-key.pem
  9. Install the app on your org/repos and note the Installation ID (from the URL after install)
  10. Update hive.yaml:
github:
  app_id: <your-app-id>
  installation_id: <your-installation-id>
  key_file: /secrets/gh-app-key.pem
  oauth_client_id: <your-client-id>

Restart the container after updating.

Required Permissions (Repository)
  • Contents: Read & write (clone, push branches)
  • Issues: Read & write (create/close issues)
  • Pull requests: Read & write (create/merge PRs)
  • Metadata: Read-only (required by GitHub)
  • Checks: Read-only (monitor CI status)
  • Actions: Read-only (monitor workflow runs)
Required Permissions (Organization)
  • Members: Read-only (identify contributors)
🐝 Hive Chat

ACMM Maturity Level

Select your AI-native Continuous Maturity Model (ACMM) level. Each level provides a curated set of agents with increasing autonomy. Agents are additive — higher levels build on lower ones.

What is Hive?

Hive is an open source AI Agent Orchestration system where AI agents autonomously maintain software repos. It’s configurable for any GitHub repository. A governor controls agent pace based on queue depth. This dashboard shows it running live on KubeStellar.

Agents

  • Scanner — triages issues, dispatches fixes, merges PRs
  • Guide — architecture analysis, code walkthroughs, advisory reviews
  • CI-Maintainer — CI health, workflow fixes, GA4 monitoring
  • Architect — cross-cutting RFCs, refactors, new features
  • Outreach — ADOPTERS outreach, community engagement
  • Sec-Check — security gate on PRs, contributor vetting
  • Quality — test coverage, integration tests, quality gates
  • Strategist — experiment design, A/B testing, strategy lab
  • Supervisor — monitors all agents, detects stalls, reports status
